mechanism: Inhibits Janus kinases (JAK1 and JAK2), disrupting signaling pathways associated with cell proliferation and survival.
Alkylates DNA, leading to cross-linking and subsequent cell death.
Inhibits bacterial DNA synthesis; potential antigrowth effects in cancer cells.
Activates AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK), inhibiting gluconeogenesis and promoting apoptosis in cancer cells.
Inhibits DNA and RNA synthesis, with potential apoptotic effects in tumor cells.
Inhibits angiogenesis and has immune-modulating effects that can promote apoptosis in cancer cells.
Inhibits proteasome activity, leading to accumulation of pro-apoptotic factors and cell cycle arrest.
Inhibits poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP), leading to impaired DNA repair and increased cell death in BRCA-deficient cells.
Inhibits the receptor tyrosine kinases HER2 and EGFR, disrupting pathways involved in cell proliferation.
Inhibits DNA synthesis and repair, leading to apoptosis in rapidly dividing cells.
Inhibits vascular endothelial growth factor receptors (VEGFRs), disrupting angiogenesis and tumor growth.
May have roles in promoting nitric oxide production and influencing metabolic pathways.
Inhibits calcineurin, leading to decreased production of interleukin-2 and other cytokines.
Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases, blocking pathways that promote cancer cell proliferation and angiogenesis.
Induces apoptosis through NF-kB inhibition and generation of reactive oxygen species (ROS).
